roadmap.goal: Enforce the 2-page CV contract at the render seam: compile_cv fails closed when the tectonic transcript reports more than max_pages (default 2) or reports no page count at all, so an overflowing CV can never be shipped as success again.
roadmap.note: Bugfix via /nw-bugfix from docs/feature/fix-3-page-cv-overflow/rca.md (the authoritative spec, incl. its 'Regression tests' list). No DISTILL .feature scenarios; regression unit tests through the existing CommandRunner port (ADR-0002, hexagonal seam) are the acceptance net. Two steps: RED (regression tests, core test fails on current code) then GREEN (page-count assertion).

validation.rationale: Review 1: 4 blockers. Re-review with code evidence: issues 1/3/4 WITHDRAWN (category errors - roadmap steps describe future work). Surviving issue 2 was the same category error (faults 01-01 for not yet containing the constructor 01-01 adds) - overruled; its real substance (run_capturing success path suppresses stderr) folded into 01-01 criterion 3.

steps.name: Regression tests for the page-count contract (RED)
criteria: New FakeRunner-based unit tests live in src/file_handlers.rs's existing #[cfg(test)] mod tests (project convention: tests colocated in src), covering rca.md regression items 1-5: a transcript reporting 3 pages makes compile_cv return Err; a 2-page transcript returns Ok; a transcript with no page line returns Err (fail closed); a page line arriving on stderr instead of stdout is still honored; max_pages configured to 1 makes a 2-page transcript fail.

The core regression test (3-page transcript => compile_cv Err) demonstrably FAILS against the current unfixed compile_cv, which treats builder exit 0 as success (RED evidence captured in the step's test run).

THIS STEP ADDS FakeRunner::with_stderr to src/command_runner.rs's testing module: a SUCCESSFUL run whose captured stderr carries the given transcript text. Note the existing run_capturing success path forces stderr to String::new() - it must be changed to surface self.stderr on success (existing callers inject stderr only on failure, so they stay green). Test-support code only, no production behavior change in this step.
Pre-existing tests are left untouched; rca.md item 6 (the existing e2e test_prepare_cv_end_to_end_with_fake_builder) is intentionally deferred to step 01-02, where the fix makes it observable.

steps.name: Page-count assertion in compile_cv (GREEN)
criteria: compile_cv invokes the build through the runner's capturing run (run_capturing on the CommandRunner port) with a configurable transcript-probe just-variable override, default 'tectonic=tectonic --print', so the TeX transcript reaches the captured streams.

The LAST 'Output written on ... (N pages' match across stdout+stderr combined decides the outcome: Err when N exceeds max_pages, Err when no match is found at all (fail closed), Ok otherwise; the error names the offending page count and the allowed maximum.
BuildConfig gains max_pages (default 2) and the probe override, both read from the [build] section of the config INI; both keys are documented in rusty-cv-config-example.ini.
The existing e2e test_prepare_cv_end_to_end_with_fake_builder stays green: PdfWritingRunner in src/main.rs's test module emits a 2-page transcript line (rca.md regression item 6).
All six rca.md regression items pass and the entire suite is green (cargo nextest run and cargo test), with clippy -D warnings clean.
steps.scenario_name: n/a bugfix — rca.md §Regression tests items 1-6 green
